title: Rules and Regulations on the Employment of Seafarers Onboard Philippine Registered Ships Engaged in International Voyage - Compliance and Enforcement
content:
SECTION 1. Compliance and Enforcement - Pursuant to Article 128 of the Labor Code, as amended, the Secretary or his/her duly authorized representative shall have access to all ships and conduct inspection to ensure compliance with working and living standards as provided herein. The DOLE Regional Office or Philippine Overseas Labor Office (POLO) having jurisdiction over the port of call of the ship shall conduct the inspection and issue the corresponding Maritime Labour Certificate.

The Secretary may authorize recognized organizations (ROs) such as members of the International Association of Classification Societies (IACS) and other reputable international classification organizations presently performing classification of ships in international trade to conduct inspection of ships and issue the Maritime Labour Certificate.

SECTION 2. Issuance of DMLC Parts I and II - The DMLC Part I shall be issued by the Secretary within fifteen (15) days from the effectivity of these Rules and Regulations. The DMLC Part II shall be accomplished by the shipowner and certified by the Secretary or his/her authorized representative.

SECTION 3. Inspection and Certification:
- 3.1. All ships are required to carry and maintain a Declaration of Maritime Labour Compliance (DMLC) and Maritime Labour Certificate. DMLC Part I is issued by the Secretary or authorized representative; DMLC Part II is accomplished by the shipowner and certified by the Secretary or representative. The Maritime Labour Certificate certifies compliance with Philippine laws and regulations regarding seafarers' working conditions and must be posted conspicuously onboard. DMLC Parts I and II shall be attached to the Certificate.
- 3.2. Validity of Maritime Labour Certificates and Intermediate Inspection - Certificates are valid for five (5) years, subject to an intermediate inspection between the second and third anniversary dates.
- 3.3. Renewal of Certificates - Shipowners must file renewal applications ninety (90) days prior to expiration with the appropriate Regional Office, POLO, or authorized RO, providing details of the ship's availability for inspection.
- 3.4. Revocation of Maritime Labour Certificate - Grounds include unremedied violations, incomplete inspections due to shipowner fault, change of ship flag, cessation of shipowner responsibility, invalid or revoked seaworthiness certificates, and other analogous grounds determined by the Secretary.
- 3.5. Unscheduled/Spot Inspection - May be ordered by the Secretary or authorized representatives upon reports of violations, imminent danger, accidents, or as deemed necessary.
- 3.6. Report of Inspection - Inspection reports are submitted to the Secretary with copies to the ship's Master; reports are posted onboard and may be provided to seafarers' representatives upon request.
- 3.7. Prohibition to Leave Port - Ships without valid Maritime Labour Certificates may be prevented from leaving port by the Philippine Coast Guard or notified to port State control authorities if outside Philippine territory.

SECTION 4. Disposition of Cases - Violations found during inspections shall be resolved according to applicable labor case rules and regulations.

SECTION 5. Manual of Inspection and Certification - A DOLE Manual will be issued by the BWC to provide uniform guidance to Regional Offices, POLO, authorized ROs, seafarers, and shipowners.

SECTION 6. Onboard Complaint Procedures - Grievance procedures per the POEA SEC shall be observed; where a Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA) exists, grievance settlement follows the agreed procedures.

SECTION 7. Onshore Complaints - Seafarers may avail of DOLE conciliation-mediation services by filing requests at POEA or DOLE Regional/Field/Provincial or POLO offices.
